steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Alegado and colleagues concluded that both A. ratkowskyi (Bowman's bacterium) and A. boritolerans (Ahmed's bacterium) might have played a role in multicellular development. A supporting finding must show that S. rosetta responded to each bacterium — one researcher's result alone is not enough.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Bowman and Ahmed found that *S. rosetta* tended to form colonies after bacterial exposure.": Both researchers found the same colony-forming response, one for each bacterium. This directly supports a conclusion about both bacteria. ✓
  • "Bowman found that *S. rosetta* tended to form colonies after bacterial exposure, but Ahmed did not.": Only one bacterium triggered a response. This undermines the "both" conclusion rather than supporting it. ✗
  • "Ahmed found that *S. rosetta* tended to form colonies after bacterial exposure, but Bowman did not.": Same problem as B in reverse — only one bacterium produced the effect, so the "both" conclusion is unsupported. ✗
  • "Neither Bowman nor Ahmed found that *S. rosetta* tended to form colonies after bacterial exposure.": No colony-forming response at all. This contradicts rather than supports Alegado's conclusion. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
